message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### C# API Design: Resumable Model Benchmark Runner

Given the operational constraint of **one heavy model server at a time**, the architecture must prioritize sequential execution of model-dependent tasks while allowing asynchronous management and state persistence for resumability.

#### 1. Core API Sketch

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

public record BenchmarkResult(BenchmarkContext Context, double Score, DateTime Timestamp);

public interface IBenchmarkRepository
{
    Task<IEnumerable<BenchmarkResult>> GetCompletedResultsAsync();
    Task SaveResultAsync(BenchmarkResult result);
    Task SaveProgressAsync(string jobId, int lastCompletedIndex);
    Task<int> GetLastCompletedIndexAsync(string jobId);
}

public interface IModelRunner
{
    // Orchestrates the Docker harness execution
    Task<double[]> RunExperimentAsync(BenchmarkContext context, CancellationToken ct);
}

public class BenchmarkEngine
{
    private readonly IModelRunner _runner;
    private readonly IBenchmarkRepository _repo;
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _modelLock = new(1, 1); // Ensures one heavy model at a time

    public BenchmarkEngine(IModelRunner runner, IBenchmarkRepository repo)
    {
        _runner = runner;
        _repo = repo;
    }

    public async Task RunSuiteAsync(string jobId, List<BenchmarkContext> suite, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        int startIndex = await _repo.GetLastCompletedIndexAsync(jobId);

        for (int i = startIndex; i < suite.Count; i++)
        {
            ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
            var context = suite[i];

            // Critical Section:
